description_long: This object is a large, complex solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a grid-like pattern. The panels are mounted on a robust, metallic support structure with visible framing and several raised sections, likely for optimizing sunlight exposure. The surface includes both dark photovoltaic cells and lighter, metallic or composite areas, with some blue-tinted sections that may represent protective coverings or electrical components. The installation appears to be designed for a flat or gently sloped surface, possibly a rooftop or a dedicated ground platform. The structure is segmented, with some areas elevated and others recessed, suggesting integrated electrical or maintenance access points. The overall design is industrial and functional, suitable for urban environments such as commercial rooftops or solar farms.
description: A large, segmented solar energy installation with multiple grid-arranged panels on a metallic support structure, featuring both dark photovoltaic and blue-tinted sections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grid of solar panels and the raised, segmented structure with blue and black sections.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the varying elevations of the panels and the extended support structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the installation and the arrangement of blue-tinted and black panel sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the stepped design and the distribution of panels across the structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly discernible.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or ground-mounted with a metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame structure.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows in a staggered grid configuration, approximately 8-10 panels in total, with some panels elevated above others.
